channel.title: Server Security on Mahmoud - Penetration Tester | System Admin
channel.description: Recent content in Server Security on Mahmoud - Penetration Tester | System Admin

item.title: Hardening Linux Servers: A Production-Ready Checklist
item.description: When setting up a new Linux server—whether it’s a web server, database, or application host—security hardening is non-negotiable. This guide covers the essential steps I follow when preparing any production Linux server. Initial Setup Create a Non-Root Admin User Never use root for daily operations. Create a dedicated admin user: # Create user with sudo privileges useradd -m -s /bin/bash sysadmin usermod -aG sudo sysadmin # Debian/Ubuntu usermod -aG wheel sysadmin # RHEL/CentOS # Set a strong password passwd sysadmin # Configure sudo without password (optional, for automation) echo "sysadmin ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL" > /etc/sudoers.
